raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Identifies the load-bearing concept (the prevailing view on SGD) and shows where the draft drifts from it by mischaracterizing the community consensus and omitting key results.",
    "R1.2": "Walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, proposes minimal repairs (e.g., clarifying assumptions, providing empirical evidence, citing prior work).",
    "R1.4": "Names one prior work (Jin et al. 2017) and articulates the delta (polylogarithmic dimension dependence vs linear-per-iteration).",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence.",
    "RX.3": "Mostly within scope with one drift (missing in the introduction section).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (overreaching claims about the field and the algorithm class).",
    "RX.5": "Output blends sources without traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.95,
    "R1.4": 0.85,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 1,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Identifies the load-bearing concept (the prevailing view on SGD) and shows where the draft drifts from it by mischaracterizing the community consensus and omitting key results.
rationale.R1.2: Walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, proposes minimal repairs (e.g., clarifying assumptions, providing empirical evidence, citing prior work).
rationale.R1.4: Names one prior work (Jin et al. 2017) and articulates the delta (polylogarithmic dimension dependence vs linear-per-iteration).
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope with one drift (missing in the introduction section).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (overreaching claims about the field and the algorithm class).
